preview_text:
The model favors Saudi Arabia at 40.5%, making them narrow favorites on a neutral ground. Cape Verde's win probability registers at 28.4%, leaving them as the underdog but with a real path to victory that reflects meaningful uncertainty rather than elimination.

The draw outcome carries notable weight at 31.1% in the model's forecast, suggesting this match may prove competitive and tightly contested. The spread across outcomes is relatively even — no single result dominates decisively — which indicates a genuinely close matchup where tactical execution and efficiency will likely prove decisive. A draw remains plausible.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge in a material way. Bookmakers assign Saudi Arabia a lower win probability (34.4%) while backing Cape Verde more heavily (36.8%), a gap of roughly 8 percentage points favoring the underdog in market pricing. Polymarket odds align more closely with the bookmaker view. This dashboard presents both signals without claiming which assessment proves more accurate.
